Name: curvature_aware_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing via Deterministic Optimization Proxies
Short Hypothesis: By explicitly quantifying curvature-aware precision routing through deterministic optimization proxies, we can achieve computational efficiency and numerical stability in single-GPU deployments without heuristic fragility.
Related Work: While Spectral Latent Steering (SLS) focuses on zero-training test-time optimization via low-rank subspace projection, our proposal diverges by emphasizing deterministic optimization proxies and explicit numerical stability analysis. Unlike SLS, which relies on PCA for subspace identification, our approach integrates spectral quantization theory with curvature-aware diagnostics to formalize precision routing protocols. This ensures strict computational overhead bounds and deterministic execution pathways, distinguishing our work from heuristic-based methods.
Abstract: We propose a curvature-aware precision routing framework that leverages deterministic optimization proxies to achieve computational efficiency and numerical stability in single-GPU deployments. By integrating spectral quantization theory with curvature-aware diagnostics, we formalize precision routing protocols that replace heuristic fragility claims with explicit condition number constraints and PAC-Bayes generalization bounds. Our methodology enforces coarse-grained, block-level precision tiering to guarantee strict overhead bounds and preserve deterministic execution pathways. Empirical validation includes systematic ablation protocols, multi-seed variance quantification, and formal PAC-Bayes verification. Our approach bridges theoretical ML with scalable inference, ensuring resource efficiency and mathematical transparency.
Experiments: Implement deterministic optimization proxies for curvature-aware precision routing.
Conduct systematic ablation studies to evaluate the impact of block-level precision tiering on computational efficiency and numerical stability.
Perform multi-seed variance quantification to assess the robustness of the proposed framework.
Validate the approach using formal PAC-Bayes verification and compare against heuristic-based methods.
Evaluate the framework on benchmark datasets for mathematical reasoning and code generation tasks, measuring computational overhead and numerical stability.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential complexity in integrating spectral quantization theory with curvature-aware diagnostics.
Risk of increased computational overhead due to deterministic optimization proxies.
Challenges in ensuring numerical stability across diverse datasets and tasks.
